metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students explore robot design, programming, and control systems, integrating mechanical, electrical, and software engineering. This course emphasizes practical robotics applications, sensor integration, and development of autonomous systems.
metadata.general_description: An applied course in robot design and programming, sensors, automation, and competition robotics.

big_ideas.statement: Robots integrate mechanical systems, electrical components, and software to accomplish automated tasks and complex movements.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding mechanical design, electrical circuits, and programming enables creation of functional robotic systems.

big_ideas.statement: Sensors provide robots with environmental awareness, enabling responses to conditions and autonomous operation.
big_ideas.elaboration: Proper sensor selection, integration, and interpretation of sensor data are critical for robot functionality and performance.

big_ideas.statement: Robot programming and control systems translate intended behaviours into sequences of commands and responses.
big_ideas.elaboration: Proficiency in robotics programming, algorithms, and control logic enables development of increasingly sophisticated robotic behaviours.

big_ideas.statement: Robotics design and implementation require systematic problem-solving, iterative testing, and refinement.
big_ideas.elaboration: Structured design approaches, testing procedures, and team collaboration lead to successful robotic solutions.

competencies.raw: Design robots to accomplish specific tasks and functions
i_can_statements: I can analyze task requirements and constraints
I can design mechanical systems for robots
I can design appropriate actuators and motors
I can plan robot configurations and movements

competencies.raw: Plan sensor integration and control systems
i_can_statements: I can select appropriate sensors for applications
I can design sensor arrangements and placement
I can plan control systems and feedback
I can design autonomous operation logic
I can plan robot algorithms and logic
I can design state machines and decision trees
I can plan sensor-driven behaviours
I can optimize robot performance

competencies.raw: Evaluate robot performance and recommend improvements
i_can_statements: I can test robot functionality against specifications
I can identify performance issues
I can analyze robot behaviour and efficiency
I can recommend design and programming improvements

competencies.raw: Build robot mechanical systems and structures
i_can_statements: I can construct robot chassis and frames
I can assemble robot components and systems
I can perform mechanical calibration and alignment
I can maintain robot mechanical integrity

competencies.raw: Install and configure robot electrical and sensor systems
i_can_statements: I can install motors and actuators
I can integrate sensors and connections
I can configure power systems
I can troubleshoot electrical connections

competencies.raw: Program robots using robotics languages and platforms
i_can_statements: I can write robot control programs
I can implement sensor-based logic
I can program autonomous behaviours
I can debug robot programs
I can conduct robot testing procedures
I can measure and analyze robot performance
I can identify and resolve issues
I can optimize for efficiency and accuracy

competencies.raw: Use robotics platforms and programming environments
i_can_statements: I can use robotics platforms effectively
I can use robotics programming environments
I can understand common robotics frameworks
I can configure and customize robotics systems
I can select and use appropriate sensors
I can interface sensors with robot controllers
I can select appropriate motors and actuators
I can calibrate and test sensors

competencies.raw: Apply control systems and automation concepts
i_can_statements: I can understand feedback control systems
I can implement PID control
I can design state machines
I can program autonomous decision-making
I can assess new robotics platforms and tools
I can understand AI and machine learning in robotics
I can evaluate swarm robotics and collaboration
I can identify robotics applications and innovations

areas.name: Robot Design and Mechanics
topics: Mechanical design: frames, joints, and linkages

areas.name: Robot Programming and Algorithms
topics: Control flow: loops, conditionals, state machines
